playbook.intent: Sync-first ceremony chaos -> async-first rhythm with sync events only when blocking.
playbook.scope: PM is running a P4 outsource engagement where the dev team and client share <2h overlap. This playbook sets up the recurring cadence: async standups, async sprint planning, recorded demos, async retros, and an AI-assisted client status digest. Sync events are scheduled only when a decision is blocked. Done when one full sprint has completed without a missed deliverable and the client opted into the async cadence.

success-criteria.criterion: All five ceremonies running async-first for one full sprint
Sync events restricted to blocking decisions (<2 per sprint)
Client confirms async cadence is acceptable in writing
No deliverable missed during the first async sprint

stage.name: Map overlap
stage.intent: Know exactly when sync is even possible.

tasks.task: Plot working hours per person across all timezones
Mark non-overlap blocks as async-only territory

stage.decision-gate: Advance only after the team agrees on the overlap window.

stage.name: Convert standups
stage.intent: Daily standup as written thread, not a meeting.

tasks.task: Stand up a daily async-standup thread in Slack/Teams
Require posts before each contributor's local 11:00

stage.decision-gate: Advance when 5 consecutive days have full participation.

stage.name: Convert planning + retros
stage.intent: Sprint planning and retro across timezones without a 2h meeting.

tasks.task: Pre-fill sprint plan template; team comments + votes async over 24h
Run async retro with structured prompts collected over 48h
Reserve 30 min sync only for unresolved planning conflicts

stage.decision-gate: Advance only when first cycle of async planning + retro completes inside SLA.

stage.name: Recorded demos
stage.intent: Client sees progress on their own time.

tasks.task: Record 5-10 min Loom-style demo at end of sprint
Post in client channel with embedded next-step questions
Schedule sync demo only if client requests decisions

stage.decision-gate: Advance when client responds to demo without requesting a sync call.

stage.name: AI status digest
stage.intent: Weekly client status assembled by AI from artifacts.

tasks.task: Configure AI pipeline to pull standup threads + Jira deltas
Auto-draft weekly status; PM reviews + signs
Deliver to client on a fixed day/time

stage.decision-gate: Advance when client accepts the digest as the primary status channel.

stage.name: Lock cadence
stage.intent: Get explicit client opt-in so the cadence sticks.

tasks.task: Document the async cadence in the engagement charter

stage.decision-gate: Done when charter is signed and 30-day check-in is on the calendar.

extra.value: ['Treating async as ''whenever'' -> threads stall, decisions slip', Hidden sync calls between PM + one timezone -> other timezone feels excluded, Skipping the AI status digest -> client demands more meetings]
['Did the team complete one sprint without missing a deliverable?', 'Did the client agree in writing that async is working?', 'Are sync calls limited to genuinely blocking decisions?']

{human: faion get-content async-cross-timezone-delivery-cadence-p4-outsource --format md, agent: faion get-content async-cross-timezone-delivery-cadence-p4-outsource --format context, json: faion get-content async-cross-timezone-delivery-cadence-p4-outsource --format json}
